   473→func (r *JobRepository) HasTag(jobID int64, tagType string, tagName string) bool {
   474→	var id int64
   475→	q := sq.Select("id").From("tag").Join("jobtag ON jobtag.tag_id = tag.id").
   476→		Where("jobtag.job_id = ?", jobID).Where("tag.tag_type = ?", tagType).
   477→		Where("tag.tag_name = ?", tagName)
   478→	err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).QueryRow().Scan(&id)
   479→	if err != nil {
   480→		return false
   481→	} else {
   482→		return true
   483→	}
   484→}

   507→// GetTags returns a list of all scoped tags if job is nil or of the tags that the job with that database ID has.
   508→func (r *JobRepository) GetTags(user *schema.User, job *int64) ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
   509→	q := sq.Select("id", "tag_type", "tag_name", "tag_scope").From("tag")
   510→	if job != nil {
   511→		q = q.Join("jobtag ON jobtag.tag_id = tag.id").Where("jobtag.job_id = ?", *job)
   512→	}
   513→
   514→	rows, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   515→	if err != nil {
   516→		s, _, _ := q.ToSql()
   517→		cclog.Errorf("Error get tags with %s: %v", s, err)
   518→		return nil, err
   519→	}
   520→	defer rows.Close()
   521→
   522→	tags := make([]*schema.Tag, 0)
   523→	for rows.Next() {
   524→		tag := &schema.Tag{}
   525→		if err := rows.Scan(&tag.ID, &tag.Type, &tag.Name, &tag.Scope); err != nil {
   526→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning tag rows in GetTags: %v", err)
   527→			return nil, err
   528→		}
   529→		// Handle Scope Filtering: Tag Scope is Global, Private (== Username) or User is auth'd to view Admin Tags
   530→		readable, err := r.checkScopeAuth(user, "read", tag.Scope)
   531→		if err != nil {
   532→			return nil, err
   533→		}
   534→		if readable {
   535→			tags = append(tags, tag)
   536→		}
   537→	}
   538→
   539→	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
   540→		return nil, err
   541→	}
   542→
   543→	return tags, nil
   544→}

   620→func (r *JobRepository) ImportTag(jobID int64, tagType string, tagName string, tagScope string) (err error) {
   621→	// Import has no scope ctx, only import from metafile to DB (No recursive archive update required), only returns err
   622→
   623→	tagID, exists := r.TagID(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   624→	if !exists {
   625→		tagID, err = r.CreateTag(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   626→		if err != nil {
   627→			return err
   628→		}
   629→	}
   630→
   631→	q := sq.Insert("jobtag").Columns("job_id", "tag_id").Values(jobID, tagID)
   632→
   633→	if _, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   634→		s, _, _ := q.ToSql()
   635→		cclog.Errorf("Error adding tag on import with %s: %v", s, err)
   636→		return err
   637→	}
   638→
   639→	return nil
   640→}

   642→// checkScopeAuth validates whether a user is authorized to perform an operation on a tag with the given scope.
   643→//
   644→// This function implements the tag scope authorization matrix:
   645→//
   646→//	Scope        | Read Access                      | Write Access
   647→//	-------------|----------------------------------|----------------------------------
   648→//	"global"     | All users                        | Admin, Support, API-only
   649→//	"admin"      | Admin, Support                   | Admin, API-only
   650→//	<username>   | Owner only                       | Owner only (private tags)
   651→//
   652→// Parameters:
   653→//   - user: User attempting the operation (must not be nil)
   654→//   - operation: Either "read" or "write"
   655→//   - scope: Tag scope value ("global", "admin", or username for private tags)
   656→//
   657→// Returns:
   658→//   - pass: true if authorized, false if denied
   659→//   - err: error only if operation is invalid or user is nil
   660→//
   661→// Special cases:
   662→//   - API-only users (single role: RoleApi) can write to admin and global scopes for automation
   663→//   - Private tags use the username as scope, granting exclusive access to that user
   664→func (r *JobRepository) checkScopeAuth(user *schema.User, operation string, scope string) (pass bool, err error) {
   665→	if user != nil {
   666→		switch {
   667→		case operation == "write" && scope == "admin":
   668→			if user.HasRole(schema.RoleAdmin) || (len(user.Roles) == 1 && user.HasRole(schema.RoleAPI)) {
   669→				return true, nil
   670→			}
   671→			return false, nil
   672→		case operation == "write" && scope == "global":
   673→			if user.HasAnyRole([]schema.Role{schema.RoleAdmin, schema.RoleSupport}) || (len(user.Roles) == 1 && user.HasRole(schema.RoleAPI)) {
   674→				return true, nil
   675→			}
   676→			return false, nil
   677→		case operation == "write" && scope == user.Username:
   678→			return true, nil
   679→		case operation == "read" && scope == "admin":
   680→			return user.HasAnyRole([]schema.Role{schema.RoleAdmin, schema.RoleSupport}), nil
   681→		case operation == "read" && scope == "global":
   682→			return true, nil
   683→		case operation == "read" && scope == user.Username:
   684→			return true, nil
   685→		default:
   686→			if operation == "read" || operation == "write" {
   687→				// No acceptable scope: deny tag
   688→				return false, nil
   689→			} else {
   690→				return false, fmt.Errorf("error while checking tag operation auth: unknown operation (%s)", operation)
   691→			}
   692→		}
   693→	} else {
   694→		return false, fmt.Errorf("error while checking tag operation auth: no user in context")
   695→	}
   696→}

    20→// Find executes a SQL query to find a specific batch job.
    21→// The job is queried using the batch job id, the cluster name,
    22→// and the start time of the job in UNIX epoch time seconds.
    23→// It returns a pointer to a schema.Job data structure and an error variable.
    24→// To check if no job was found test err == sql.ErrNoRows
    25→func (r *JobRepository) Find(
    26→	jobID *int64,
    27→	cluster *string,
    28→	startTime *int64,
    29→) (*schema.Job, error) {
    30→	if jobID == nil {
    31→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("jobID cannot be nil")
    32→	}
    33→
    34→	start := time.Now()
    35→	q := sq.Select(jobColumns...).From("job").
    36→		Where("job.job_id = ?", *jobID)
    37→
    38→	if cluster != nil {
    39→		q = q.Where("job.cluster = ?", *cluster)
    40→	}
    41→	if startTime != nil {
    42→		q = q.Where("job.start_time = ?", *startTime)
    43→	}
    44→
    45→	q = q.OrderBy("job.id DESC").Limit(1) // always use newest matching job by db id if more than one match
    46→
    47→	cclog.Debugf("Timer Find %s", time.Since(start))
    48→	return scanJob(q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).QueryRow())
    49→}
    50→
    51→// FindCached executes a SQL query to find a specific batch job from the job_cache table.
    52→// The job is queried using the batch job id, and optionally filtered by cluster name
    53→// and start time (UNIX epoch time seconds). This method uses cached job data which
    54→// may be stale but provides faster access than Find().
    55→// It returns a pointer to a schema.Job data structure and an error variable.
    56→// To check if no job was found test err == sql.ErrNoRows
    57→func (r *JobRepository) FindCached(
    58→	jobID *int64,
    59→	cluster *string,
    60→	startTime *int64,
    61→) (*schema.Job, error) {
    62→	if jobID == nil {
    63→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("jobID cannot be nil")
    64→	}
    65→
    66→	q := sq.Select(jobCacheColumns...).From("job_cache").
    67→		Where("job_cache.job_id = ?", *jobID)
    68→
    69→	if cluster != nil {
    70→		q = q.Where("job_cache.cluster = ?", *cluster)
    71→	}
    72→	if startTime != nil {
    73→		q = q.Where("job_cache.start_time = ?", *startTime)
    74→	}
    75→
    76→	q = q.OrderBy("job_cache.id DESC").Limit(1) // always use newest matching job by db id if more than one match
    77→
    78→	return scanJob(q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).QueryRow())
    79→}
    80→
    81→// FindAll executes a SQL query to find all batch jobs matching the given criteria.
    82→// Jobs are queried using the batch job id, and optionally filtered by cluster name
    83→// and start time (UNIX epoch time seconds).
    84→// It returns a slice of pointers to schema.Job data structures and an error variable.
    85→// An empty slice is returned if no matching jobs are found.
    86→func (r *JobRepository) FindAll(
    87→	jobID *int64,
    88→	cluster *string,
    89→	startTime *int64,
    90→) ([]*schema.Job, error) {
    91→	if jobID == nil {
    92→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("jobID cannot be nil")
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	start := time.Now()
    96→	q := sq.Select(jobColumns...).From("job").
    97→		Where("job.job_id = ?", *jobID)
    98→
    99→	if cluster != nil {
   100→		q = q.Where("job.cluster = ?", *cluster)
   101→	}
   102→	if startTime != nil {
   103→		q = q.Where("job.start_time = ?", *startTime)
   104→	}
   105→
   106→	rows, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   107→	if err != nil {
   108→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running FindAll query for jobID=%d: %v", *jobID, err)
   109→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to execute FindAll query: %w", err)
   110→	}
   111→	defer rows.Close()
   112→
   113→	jobs := make([]*schema.Job, 0, 10)
   114→	for rows.Next() {
   115→		job, err := scanJob(rows)
   116→		if err != nil {
   117→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning rows in FindAll: %v", err)
   118→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to scan job row: %w", err)
   119→		}
   120→		jobs = append(jobs, job)
   121→	}
   122→	cclog.Debugf("Timer FindAll %s", time.Since(start))
   123→	return jobs, nil
   124→}
   125→
   126→// GetJobList returns job IDs for non-running jobs.
   127→// This is useful to process large job counts and intended to be used
   128→// together with FindById to process jobs one by one.
   129→// Use limit and offset for pagination. Use limit=0 to get all results (not recommended for large datasets).
   130→func (r *JobRepository) GetJobList(limit int, offset int) ([]int64, error) {
   131→	query := sq.Select("id").From("job").
   132→		Where("job.job_state != 'running'")
   133→
   134→	// Add pagination if limit is specified
   135→	if limit > 0 {
   136→		query = query.Limit(uint64(limit)).Offset(uint64(offset))
   137→	}
   138→
   139→	rows, err := query.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   140→	if err != nil {
   141→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running GetJobList query (limit=%d, offset=%d): %v", limit, offset, err)
   142→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to execute GetJobList query: %w", err)
   143→	}
   144→	defer rows.Close()
   145→
   146→	jl := make([]int64, 0, 1000)
   147→	for rows.Next() {
   148→		var id int64
   149→		err := rows.Scan(&id)
   150→		if err != nil {
   151→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning rows in GetJobList: %v", err)
   152→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to scan job ID: %w", err)
   153→		}
   154→		jl = append(jl, id)
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	cclog.Debugf("JobRepository.GetJobList(): Return job count %d", len(jl))
   158→	return jl, nil
   159→}
   160→
   161→// FindByID executes a SQL query to find a specific batch job.
   162→// The job is queried using the database id.
   163→// It returns a pointer to a schema.Job data structure and an error variable.
   164→// To check if no job was found test err == sql.ErrNoRows
   165→func (r *JobRepository) FindByID(ctx context.Context, jobID int64) (*schema.Job, error) {
   166→	q := sq.Select(jobColumns...).
   167→		From("job").Where("job.id = ?", jobID)
   168→
   169→	q, qerr := SecurityCheck(ctx, q)
   170→	if qerr != nil {
   171→		return nil, qerr
   172→	}
   173→
   174→	return scanJob(q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).QueryRow())
   175→}
   176→
   177→// FindByIDWithUser executes a SQL query to find a specific batch job.
   178→// The job is queried using the database id. The user is passed directly,
   179→// instead as part of the context.
   180→// It returns a pointer to a schema.Job data structure and an error variable.
   181→// To check if no job was found test err == sql.ErrNoRows
   182→func (r *JobRepository) FindByIDWithUser(user *schema.User, jobID int64) (*schema.Job, error) {
   183→	q := sq.Select(jobColumns...).
   184→		From("job").Where("job.id = ?", jobID)
   185→
   186→	q, qerr := SecurityCheckWithUser(user, q)
   187→	if qerr != nil {
   188→		return nil, qerr
   189→	}
   190→
   191→	return scanJob(q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).QueryRow())
   192→}

   194→// FindByIDDirect executes a SQL query to find a specific batch job.
   195→// The job is queried using the database id.
   196→// It returns a pointer to a schema.Job data structure and an error variable.
   197→// To check if no job was found test err == sql.ErrNoRows
   198→func (r *JobRepository) FindByIDDirect(jobID int64) (*schema.Job, error) {

   203→
   204→// FindByJobID executes a SQL query to find a specific batch job.
   205→// The job is queried using the slurm id and the clustername.
   206→// It returns a pointer to a schema.Job data structure and an error variable.
   207→// To check if no job was found test err == sql.ErrNoRows
   208→func (r *JobRepository) FindByJobID(ctx context.Context, jobID int64, startTime int64, cluster string) (*schema.Job, error) {
   209→	q := sq.Select(jobColumns...).
   210→		From("job").

   212→		Where("job.cluster = ?", cluster).
   213→		Where("job.start_time = ?", startTime)

   215→	q, qerr := SecurityCheck(ctx, q)

   223→// IsJobOwner checks if the specified user owns the batch job identified by jobID,
   224→// startTime, and cluster. Returns true if the user is the owner, false otherwise.
   225→// This method does not return errors; it returns false for both non-existent jobs
   226→// and jobs owned by other users.
   227→func (r *JobRepository) IsJobOwner(jobID int64, startTime int64, user string, cluster string) bool {
   228→	q := sq.Select("id").
   229→		From("job").
   230→		Where("job.job_id = ?", jobID).
   231→		Where("job.hpc_user = ?", user).
   232→		Where("job.cluster = ?", cluster).
   233→		Where("job.start_time = ?", startTime)
   234→
   235→	_, err := scanJob(q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).QueryRow())
   236→	if err != nil && err != sql.ErrNoRows {
   237→		cclog.Warnf("IsJobOwner: unexpected error for jobID=%d, user=%s, cluster=%s: %v", jobID, user, cluster, err)
   238→	}
   239→	return err != sql.ErrNoRows
   240→}
   241→
   242→func (r *JobRepository) FindConcurrentJobs(
   243→	ctx context.Context,
   244→	job *schema.Job,
   245→) (*model.JobLinkResultList, error) {
   246→	if job == nil {
   247→		return nil, nil
   248→	}
   249→
   250→	query, qerr := SecurityCheck(ctx, sq.Select("job.id", "job.job_id", "job.start_time").From("job"))
   251→	if qerr != nil {
   252→		return nil, qerr
   253→	}
   254→
   255→	query = query.Where("cluster = ?", job.Cluster)
   256→
   257→	if len(job.Resources) == 0 {
   258→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("job has no resources defined")
   259→	}
   260→
   261→	var startTime int64
   262→	var stopTime int64
   263→
   264→	startTime = job.StartTime
   265→	hostname := job.Resources[0].Hostname
   266→
   267→	if job.State == schema.JobStateRunning {
   268→		stopTime = time.Now().Unix()
   269→	} else {
   270→		stopTime = startTime + int64(job.Duration)
   271→	}
   272→
   273→	// Time buffer constant for finding overlapping jobs
   274→	// overlapBufferEnd: 200s buffer at job end to account for scheduling/cleanup overlap
   275→	const overlapBufferEnd = 200
   276→
   277→	stopTimeTail := stopTime - overlapBufferEnd
   278→	startTimeFront := startTime + overlapBufferEnd
   279→
   280→	queryRunning := query.Where("job.job_state = ?", "running").
   281→		Where("job.start_time <= ?", stopTimeTail)
   282→	// Get At Least One Exact Hostname Match from JSON Resources Array in Database
   283→	queryRunning = queryRunning.Where("EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM json_each(job.resources) WHERE json_extract(value, '$.hostname') = ?)", hostname)
   284→
   285→	query = query.Where("job.job_state != ?", "running").
   286→		Where("job.start_time < ?", stopTimeTail).
   287→		Where("(job.start_time + job.duration) > ?", startTimeFront)
   288→	// Get At Least One Exact Hostname Match from JSON Resources Array in Database
   289→	query = query.Where("EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM json_each(job.resources) WHERE json_extract(value, '$.hostname') = ?)", hostname)
   290→
   291→	rows, err := query.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   292→	if err != nil {
   293→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running concurrent jobs query: %v", err)
   294→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to execute concurrent jobs query: %w", err)
   295→	}
   296→	defer rows.Close()
   297→
   298→	items := make([]*model.JobLink, 0, 10)
   299→	queryString := fmt.Sprintf("cluster=%s", job.Cluster)
   300→
   301→	for rows.Next() {
   302→		var id, jobID, startTime sql.NullInt64
   303→
   304→		if err = rows.Scan(&id, &jobID, &startTime); err != nil {
   305→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning concurrent job rows: %v", err)
   306→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to scan concurrent job row: %w", err)
   307→		}
   308→
   309→		if id.Valid {
   310→			queryString += fmt.Sprintf("&jobId=%d", int(jobID.Int64))
   311→			items = append(items,
   312→				&model.JobLink{
   313→					ID:    fmt.Sprint(id.Int64),
   314→					JobID: int(jobID.Int64),
   315→				})
   316→		}
   317→	}
   318→
   319→	rows, err = queryRunning.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   320→	if err != nil {
   321→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running concurrent running jobs query: %v", err)
   322→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to execute concurrent running jobs query: %w", err)
   323→	}
   324→	defer rows.Close()
   325→
   326→	for rows.Next() {
   327→		var id, jobID, startTime sql.NullInt64
   328→
   329→		if err := rows.Scan(&id, &jobID, &startTime); err != nil {
   330→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning running concurrent job rows: %v", err)
   331→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to scan running concurrent job row: %w", err)
   332→		}
   333→
   334→		if id.Valid {
   335→			queryString += fmt.Sprintf("&jobId=%d", int(jobID.Int64))
   336→			items = append(items,
   337→				&model.JobLink{
   338→					ID:    fmt.Sprint(id.Int64),
   339→					JobID: int(jobID.Int64),
   340→				})
   341→		}
   342→	}
   343→
   344→	cnt := len(items)
   345→
   346→	return &model.JobLinkResultList{
   347→		ListQuery: &queryString,
   348→		Items:     items,
   349→		Count:     &cnt,
   350→	}, nil
   351→}

## Report: Missing defer rows.Close() Calls

After thoroughly searching all `.Query()` and `.Queryx()` calls in `/Users/jan/prg/CC/cc-backend/internal/repository/`, I found **ONE** missing `defer close` statement:

### Missing defer close:

**File:** `/Users/jan/prg/CC/cc-backend/internal/repository/user.go`  
**Line:** 100  
**Function:** `GetLdapUsernames()`  
**Issue:** `rows` is opened with `r.DB.Query()` but never closed. There is no `defer rows.Close()` statement anywhere in the function (lines 100-117). The function loops through rows but returns without closing them.

```go
func (r *UserRepository) GetLdapUsernames() ([]string, error) {
	var users []string
	rows, err := r.DB.Query(`SELECT username FROM hpc_user WHERE hpc_user.ldap = 1`)
	if err != nil {
		cclog.Warn("Error while querying usernames")
		return nil, err
	}
	// Missing: defer rows.Close()
	
	for rows.Next() {
		var username string
		if err := rows.Scan(&username); err != nil {
			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning for user '%s'", username)
			return nil, err
		}
		users = append(users, username)
	}
	
	return users, nil
}
```

### Summary of Checked Calls:

All other `.Query()` and `.Queryx()` calls in the repository properly have `defer rows.Close()` or `defer xrows.Close()` within a few lines:

- **node.go:** 7 Query() calls - All properly closed (287, 363, 416, 504, 533, 567, 627)
- **jobQuery.go:** 1 Query() call - Properly closed (87)
- **tags.go:** 5 calls (4 Query() + 1 Queryx()) - All properly closed (282, 338, 514, 552, 595)
- **user.go:** 1 Query() call - **MISSING CLOSE (100)**; 1 other call properly closed (278)
- **userConfig.go:** 1 Query() call - Properly closed (68)
- **jobCreate.go:** 1 Query() call - Properly closed (78)
- **jobFind.go:** 5 Query() calls - All properly closed (106, 139, 291, 319 x2)
- **stats.go:** 7 Query() calls - All properly closed (233, 443, 504, 758, 832, 962)
- **job.go:** 7 Query() calls - All properly closed (427, 605, 663, 751, 787, 863, 1071)

This is a resource leak that should be fixed immediately, as database connections obtained via `.Query()` must always be explicitly closed to prevent connection pool exhaustion.

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile my findings:

## Database Index Definitions Report

### Summary of Index Definitions

I've analyzed all migration files in `/Users/jan/prg/CC/cc-backend/internal/repository/migrations/sqlite3/`. Here are the comprehensive findings:

---

### **Indexes on the `job` Table**

#### **Initial Indexes (02_add-index.up.sql):**
1. `job_stats` - (cluster, subcluster, hpc_user)
2. `job_by_user` - (hpc_user)
3. `job_by_starttime` - (start_time)
4. `job_by_job_id` - (job_id, cluster, start_time)
5. `job_list` - (cluster, job_state)
6. `job_list_user` - (hpc_user, cluster, job_state)
7. `job_list_users` - (hpc_user, job_state)
8. `job_list_users_start` - (start_time, hpc_user, job_state)

#### **Comprehensive Indexes Added in Migration 08 (08_add-footprint.up.sql):**
The migration dropped old indexes and created a much more extensive set of 80+ indexes organized by filter type:

**Cluster Filter:**
- `jobs_cluster` - (cluster)
- `jobs_cluster_user` - (cluster, hpc_user)
- `jobs_cluster_project` - (cluster, project)
- `jobs_cluster_subcluster` - (cluster, subcluster)

**Cluster Filter with Sorting:**
- `jobs_cluster_starttime` - (cluster, start_time)
- `jobs_cluster_duration` - (cluster, duration)
- `jobs_cluster_numnodes` - (cluster, num_nodes)
- `jobs_cluster_numhwthreads` - (cluster, num_hwthreads)
- `jobs_cluster_numacc` - (cluster, num_acc)
- `jobs_cluster_energy` - (cluster, energy)

**Cluster + JobState Filter:**
- `jobs_cluster_jobstate` - (cluster, job_state)
- `jobs_cluster_jobstate_user` - (cluster, job_state, hpc_user)
- `jobs_cluster_jobstate_project` - (cluster, job_state, project)

**Cluster + JobState Filter Sorting:**
- `jobs_cluster_jobstate_starttime` - (cluster, job_state, start_time)
- `jobs_cluster_jobstate_duration` - (cluster, job_state, duration)
- `jobs_cluster_jobstate_numnodes` - (cluster, job_state, num_nodes)
- `jobs_cluster_jobstate_numhwthreads` - (cluster, job_state, num_hwthreads)
- `jobs_cluster_jobstate_numacc` - (cluster, job_state, num_acc)
- `jobs_cluster_jobstate_energy` - (cluster, job_state, energy)

**JobState Filter:**
- `jobs_jobstate` - (job_state)
- `jobs_jobstate_user` - (job_state, hpc_user)
- `jobs_jobstate_project` - (job_state, project)
- `jobs_jobstate_cluster` - (job_state, cluster)

**JobState Filter Sorting:**
- `jobs_jobstate_starttime` - (job_state, start_time)
- `jobs_jobstate_duration` - (job_state, duration)
- `jobs_jobstate_numnodes` - (job_state, num_nodes)
- `jobs_jobstate_numhwthreads` - (job_state, num_hwthreads)
- `jobs_jobstate_numacc` - (job_state, num_acc)
- `jobs_jobstate_energy` - (job_state, energy)

**User Filter and Sorting:**
- `jobs_user` - (hpc_user)
- `jobs_user_starttime` - (hpc_user, start_time)
- `jobs_user_duration` - (hpc_user, duration)
- `jobs_user_numnodes` - (hpc_user, num_nodes)
- `jobs_user_numhwthreads` - (hpc_user, num_hwthreads)
- `jobs_user_numacc` - (hpc_user, num_acc)
- `jobs_user_energy` - (hpc_user, energy)

**ArrayJob Filter:**
- `jobs_arrayjobid_starttime` - (array_job_id, start_time)
- `jobs_cluster_arrayjobid_starttime` - (cluster, array_job_id, start_time)

**Standalone Sorting Indexes:**

   596→   596→func (r *JobRepository) FindColumnValues(user *schema.User, query string, table string, selectColumn string, whereColumn string) (results []string, err error) {

   604→   604→			Where(table+"."+whereColumn+" LIKE ?", fmt.Sprint("%", query, "%")).

   606→   606→		if err != nil && err != sql.ErrNoRows {

   618→   618→				results = append(results, result)

   712→   712→func (r *JobRepository) StopJobsExceedingWalltimeBy(seconds int) error {
   713→   713→	start := time.Now()
   714→   714→	currentTime := time.Now().Unix()
   715→   715→	res, err := sq.Update("job").
   716→   716→		Set("monitoring_status", schema.MonitoringStatusArchivingFailed).

   718→   718→		Set("job_state", schema.JobStateFailed).

   732→   732→	}
   733→   733→

   736→   736→	}

   583→// FindColumnValues performs a generic column lookup returning multiple matches with role-based access control.
   584→// Similar to FindColumnValue but returns all matching values instead of just the first.
   585→// Only users with admin, support, or manager roles can execute this query.
   586→//
   587→// Parameters:
   588→//   - user: User context for authorization check
   589→//   - query: Search pattern (always uses LIKE with wildcards)
   590→//   - table: Database table name to query
   591→//   - selectColumn: Column name to return in results
   592→//   - whereColumn: Column name to filter on
   593→//
   594→// Returns a slice of matching values, ErrForbidden if user lacks permission,
   595→// or ErrNotFound if no matches are found.
   596→func (r *JobRepository) FindColumnValues(user *schema.User, query string, table string, selectColumn string, whereColumn string) (results []string, err error) {
   597→	if user == nil {
   598→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("user cannot be nil")
   599→	}
   600→
   601→	emptyResult := make([]string, 0)
   602→	if user.HasAnyRole([]schema.Role{schema.RoleAdmin, schema.RoleSupport, schema.RoleManager}) {
   603→		rows, err := sq.Select(table+"."+selectColumn).Distinct().From(table).
   604→			Where(table+"."+whereColumn+" LIKE ?", fmt.Sprint("%", query, "%")).
   605→			RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   606→		if err != nil && err != sql.ErrNoRows {
   607→			cclog.Errorf("Error while querying FindColumnValues (table=%s, column=%s): %v", table, selectColumn, err)
   608→			return emptyResult, fmt.Errorf("failed to find column values: %w", err)
   609→		} else if err == nil {
   610→			defer rows.Close()
   611→			for rows.Next() {
   612→				var result string
   613→				err := rows.Scan(&result)
   614→				if err != nil {
   615→					cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning rows in FindColumnValues: %v", err)
   616→					return emptyResult, fmt.Errorf("failed to scan column value: %w", err)
   617→				}
   618→				results = append(results, result)
   619→			}
   620→			return results, nil
   621→		}
   622→		return emptyResult, ErrNotFound
   623→
   624→	} else {
   625→		cclog.Infof("Non-Admin User %s : Requested Query '%s' on table '%s' : Forbidden", user.Name, query, table)
   626→		return emptyResult, ErrForbidden
   627→	}
   628→}

   630→// Partitions returns a list of distinct cluster partitions for a given cluster.
   631→// Results are cached with a 1-hour TTL to improve performance.
   632→//
   633→// Parameters:
   634→//   - cluster: Cluster name to query partitions for
   635→//
   636→// Returns a slice of partition names or an error if the database query fails.
   637→func (r *JobRepository) Partitions(cluster string) ([]string, error) {
   638→	var err error
   639→	start := time.Now()
   640→	partitions := r.cache.Get("partitions:"+cluster, func() (any, time.Duration, int) {
   641→		parts := []string{}
   642→		if err = r.DB.Select(&parts, `SELECT DISTINCT job.cluster_partition FROM job WHERE job.cluster = ?;`, cluster); err != nil {
   643→			return nil, 0, 1000
   644→		}
   645→
   646→		return parts, 1 * time.Hour, 1
   647→	})
   648→	if err != nil {
   649→		return nil, err
   650→	}
   651→	cclog.Debugf("Timer Partitions %s", time.Since(start))
   652→	return partitions.([]string), nil
   653→}

   655→// AllocatedNodes returns a map of all subclusters to a map of hostnames to the amount of jobs running on that host.
   656→// Hosts with zero jobs running on them will not show up!
   657→func (r *JobRepository) AllocatedNodes(cluster string) (map[string]map[string]int, error) {
   658→	start := time.Now()
   659→	subclusters := make(map[string]map[string]int)
   660→	rows, err := sq.Select("resources", "subcluster").From("job").
   661→		Where("job.job_state = 'running'").
   662→		Where("job.cluster = ?", cluster).
   663→		RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   664→	if err != nil {
   665→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running AllocatedNodes query for cluster=%s: %v", cluster, err)
   666→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to query allocated nodes for cluster %s: %w", cluster, err)
   667→	}
   668→
   669→	var raw []byte
   670→	defer rows.Close()
   671→	for rows.Next() {
   672→		raw = raw[0:0]
   673→		var resources []*schema.Resource
   674→		var subcluster string
   675→		if err := rows.Scan(&raw, &subcluster); err != nil {
   676→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning rows in AllocatedNodes: %v", err)
   677→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to scan allocated nodes row: %w", err)
   678→		}
   679→		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &resources); err != nil {
   680→			cclog.Warnf("Error while unmarshaling raw resources json in AllocatedNodes: %v", err)
   681→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to unmarshal resources in AllocatedNodes: %w", err)
   682→		}
   683→
   684→		hosts, ok := subclusters[subcluster]
   685→		if !ok {
   686→			hosts = make(map[string]int)
   687→			subclusters[subcluster] = hosts
   688→		}
   689→
   690→		for _, resource := range resources {
   691→			hosts[resource.Hostname] += 1
   692→		}
   693→	}
   694→
   695→	cclog.Debugf("Timer AllocatedNodes %s", time.Since(start))
   696→	return subclusters, nil
   697→}

   699→// StopJobsExceedingWalltimeBy marks running jobs as failed if they exceed their walltime limit.
   700→// This is typically called periodically to clean up stuck or orphaned jobs.
   701→//
   702→// Jobs are marked with:
   703→//   - monitoring_status: MonitoringStatusArchivingFailed
   704→//   - duration: 0
   705→//   - job_state: JobStateFailed
   706→//
   707→// Parameters:
   708→//   - seconds: Grace period beyond walltime before marking as failed
   709→//
   710→// Returns an error if the database update fails.
   711→// Logs the number of jobs marked as failed if any were affected.
   712→func (r *JobRepository) StopJobsExceedingWalltimeBy(seconds int) error {
   713→	start := time.Now()
   714→	currentTime := time.Now().Unix()
   715→	res, err := sq.Update("job").
   716→		Set("monitoring_status", schema.MonitoringStatusArchivingFailed).
   717→		Set("duration", 0).
   718→		Set("job_state", schema.JobStateFailed).
   719→		Where("job.job_state = 'running'").
   720→		Where("job.walltime > 0").
   721→		Where("job.start_time < (? - job.walltime)", currentTime-int64(seconds)).
   722→		RunWith(r.DB).Exec()
   723→	if err != nil {
   724→		cclog.Warnf("Error while stopping jobs exceeding walltime: %v", err)
   725→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to stop jobs exceeding walltime: %w", err)
   726→	}
   727→
   728→	rowsAffected, err := res.RowsAffected()
   729→	if err != nil {
   730→		cclog.Warnf("Error while fetching affected rows after stopping due to exceeded walltime: %v", err)
   731→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get rows affected count: %w", err)
   732→	}
   733→
   734→	if rowsAffected > 0 {
   735→		cclog.Infof("%d jobs have been marked as failed due to running too long", rowsAffected)
   736→	}
   737→	cclog.Debugf("Timer StopJobsExceedingWalltimeBy %s", time.Since(start))
   738→	return nil
   739→}

774→   774→// FindRunningJobs returns all currently running jobs for a specific cluster.
   775→   775→// Filters out short-running jobs based on repoConfig.MinRunningJobDuration threshold.
   776→   776→//

   782→   782→	query := sq.Select(jobColumns...).From("job").
   783→   783→		Where("job.cluster = ?", cluster).
   784→   784→		Where("job.job_state = 'running'").

   808→   808→// UpdateDuration recalculates and updates the duration field for all running jobs.
   809→   809→// Called periodically to keep job durations current without querying individual jobs.
   810→   810→//
   811→   811→// Duration is calculated as: current_time - job.start_time
   812→   812→//
   813→   813→// Returns an error if the database update fails.
   814→   814→func (r *JobRepository) UpdateDuration() error {
   815→   815→	stmnt := sq.Update("job").
   816→   816→		Set("duration", sq.Expr("? - job.start_time", time.Now().Unix())).

   828→   828→// FindJobsBetween returns jobs within a specified time range.
   829→   829→// If startTimeBegin is 0, returns all jobs before startTimeEnd.
   830→   830→// Optionally excludes tagged jobs from results.
   831→   831→//
   832→   832→// Parameters:
   833→   833→//   - startTimeBegin: Unix timestamp for range start (use 0 for unbounded start)
   834→   834→//   - startTimeEnd: Unix timestamp for range end
   835→   835→//   - omitTagged: "none" = include all jobs, "all" = exclude any tagged jobs,
   836→   836→//     "user" = exclude jobs with user-created tags (not auto-tagger types "app"/"jobClass")
   837→   837→//
   838→   838→// Returns a slice of jobs or an error if the time range is invalid or query fails.
   839→   839→func (r *JobRepository) FindJobsBetween(startTimeBegin int64, startTimeEnd int64, omitTagged string) ([]*schema.Job, error) {
   840→   840→	var query sq.SelectBuilder
   841→   841→
   842→   842→	if startTimeBegin == startTimeEnd || startTimeBegin > startTimeEnd {
   843→   843→		return nil, errors.New("startTimeBegin is equal or larger startTimeEnd")
   844→   844→	}
   845→   845→
   846→   846→	if startTimeBegin == 0 {
   847→   847→		cclog.Infof("Find jobs before %d", startTimeEnd)
   848→   848→		query = sq.Select(jobColumns...).From("job").Where("job.start_time < ?", startTimeEnd)
   849→   849→	} else {
   850→   850→		cclog.Infof("Find jobs between %d and %d", startTimeBegin, startTimeEnd)
   851→   851→		query = sq.Select(jobColumns...).From("job").Where("job.start_time BETWEEN ? AND ?", startTimeBegin, startTimeEnd)
   852→   852→	}
   853→   853→
   854→   854→	switch omitTagged {
   855→   855→	case "all":
   856→   856→		query = query.Where("NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM jobtag WHERE jobtag.job_id = job.id)")
   857→   857→	case "user":
   858→   858→		query = query.Where("NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM jobtag JOIN tag ON tag.id = jobtag.tag_id WHERE jobtag.job_id = job.id AND tag.tag_type NOT IN ('app', 'jobClass'))")
   859→   859→	}
   860→   860→
   861→   861→	query = query.OrderBy("job.cluster ASC", "job.subcluster ASC", "job.project ASC", "job.start_time ASC")
   862→   862→
   863→   863→	rows, err := query.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   864→   864→	if err != nil {
   865→   865→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running FindJobsBetween query: %v", err)
   866→   866→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to find jobs between %d and %d: %w", startTimeBegin, startTimeEnd, err)
   867→   867→	}
   868→   868→	defer rows.Close()
   869→   869→
   870→   870→	jobs := make([]*schema.Job, 0, 50)
   871→   871→	for rows.Next() {
   872→   872→		job, err := scanJob(rows)
   873→   873→		if err != nil {
   874→   874→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning rows in FindJobsBetween: %v", err)
   875→   875→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to scan job in FindJobsBetween: %w", err)
   876→   876→		}
   877→   877→		jobs = append(jobs, job)
   878→   878→	}
   879→   879→
   880→   880→	cclog.Debugf("JobRepository.FindJobsBetween(): Return job count %d (omitTagged: %v)", len(jobs), omitTagged)
   881→   881→	return jobs, nil
   882→   882→}

   884→   884→// UpdateMonitoringStatus updates the monitoring status for a job and invalidates its cache entries.
   885→   885→// Cache invalidation affects both metadata and energy footprint to ensure consistency.
   886→   886→//
   887→   887→// Parameters:
   888→   888→//   - job: Database ID of the job to update
   889→   889→//   - monitoringStatus: New monitoring status value (see schema.MonitoringStatus constants)
   890→   890→//
   891→   891→// Returns an error if the database update fails.
   892→   892→func (r *JobRepository) UpdateMonitoringStatus(job int64, monitoringStatus int32) (err error) {
   893→   893→	// Invalidate cache entries as monitoring status affects job state
   894→   894→	r.cache.Del(fmt.Sprintf("metadata:%d", job))
   895→   895→	r.cache.Del(fmt.Sprintf("energyFootprint:%d", job))
   896→   896→
   897→   897→	stmt := sq.Update("job").
   898→   898→		Set("monitoring_status", monitoringStatus).
   899→   899→		Where("job.id = ?", job)

   901→   901→	if _, err = stmt.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   902→   902→		cclog.Errorf("Error while updating monitoring status for job %d: %v", job, err)
   903→   903→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to update monitoring status for job %d: %w", job, err)
   904→   904→	}
   905→   905→	return nil
   906→   906→}

   939→   939→// UpdateEnergy calculates and updates the energy consumption for a job.
   940→   940→// This is called for running jobs during intermediate updates or when archiving.
   941→   941→//
   942→   942→// Energy calculation formula:
   943→   943→//   - For "power" metrics: Energy (kWh) = (Power_avg * NumNodes * Duration_hours) / 1000
   944→   944→//   - For "energy" metrics: Currently not implemented (would need sum statistics)
   945→   945→//
   946→   946→// The calculation accounts for:
   947→   947→//   - Multi-node jobs: Multiplies by NumNodes to get total cluster energy
   948→   948→//   - Shared jobs: Node average is already based on partial resources, so NumNodes=1
   949→   949→//   - Unit conversion: Watts * hours / 1000 = kilowatt-hours (kWh)
   950→   950→//   - Rounding: Results rounded to 2 decimal places
   951→   951→func (r *JobRepository) UpdateEnergy(
   952→   952→	stmt sq.UpdateBuilder,
   953→   953→	jobMeta *schema.Job,
   954→   954→) (sq.UpdateBuilder, error) {
   955→   955→	sc, err := archive.GetSubCluster(jobMeta.Cluster, jobMeta.SubCluster)
   956→   956→	if err != nil {
   957→   957→		cclog.Errorf("cannot get subcluster: %s", err.Error())
   958→   958→		return stmt, err
   959→   959→	}
   960→   960→	energyFootprint := make(map[string]float64)
   961→   961→
   962→   962→	// Accumulate total energy across all energy-related metrics
   963→   963→	totalEnergy := 0.0
   964→   964→	for _, fp := range sc.EnergyFootprint {
   965→   965→		// Calculate energy for this specific metric
   966→   966→		metricEnergy := 0.0
   967→   967→		if i, err := archive.MetricIndex(sc.MetricConfig, fp); err == nil {
   968→   968→			switch sc.MetricConfig[i].Energy {
   969→   969→			case "energy": // Metric already in energy units (Joules or Wh)
   970→   970→				cclog.Warnf("Update EnergyFootprint for Job %d and Metric %s on cluster %s: Set to 'energy' in cluster.json: Not implemented, will return 0.0", jobMeta.JobID, jobMeta.Cluster, fp)
   971→   971→				// FIXME: Needs sum as stats type to accumulate energy values over time
   972→   972→			case "power": // Metric in power units (Watts)
   973→   973→				// Energy (kWh) = Power (W) × Time (h) / 1000

   783→		Where("job.cluster = ?", cluster).

   785→		Where("job.duration > ?", repoConfig.MinRunningJobDuration)

   796→		job, err := scanJob(rows)
   797→		if err != nil {
   798→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning rows in FindRunningJobs: %v", err)
   799→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to scan job in FindRunningJobs: %w", err)
   800→		}
   801→		jobs = append(jobs, job)
   802→	}
   803→
   804→	cclog.Debugf("JobRepository.FindRunningJobs(): Return job count %d (cluster: %s)", len(jobs), cluster)
   805→	return jobs, nil
   806→}
   807→
   808→// UpdateDuration recalculates and updates the duration field for all running jobs.
   809→// Called periodically to keep job durations current without querying individual jobs.
   810→//
   811→// Duration is calculated as: current_time - job.start_time
   812→//
   813→// Returns an error if the database update fails.
   814→func (r *JobRepository) UpdateDuration() error {
   815→	stmnt := sq.Update("job").
   816→		Set("duration", sq.Expr("? - job.start_time", time.Now().Unix())).
   817→		Where("job_state = 'running'")
   818→
   819→	_, err := stmnt.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec()
   820→	if err != nil {
   821→		cclog.Errorf("Error while updating duration for running jobs: %v", err)
   822→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to update duration for running jobs: %w", err)
   823→	}
   824→
   825→	return nil
   826→}
   827→
   828→// FindJobsBetween returns jobs within a specified time range.
   829→// If startTimeBegin is 0, returns all jobs before startTimeEnd.
   830→// Optionally excludes tagged jobs from results.
   831→//
   832→// Parameters:
   833→//   - startTimeBegin: Unix timestamp for range start (use 0 for unbounded start)
   834→//   - startTimeEnd: Unix timestamp for range end
   835→//   - omitTagged: "none" = include all jobs, "all" = exclude any tagged jobs,
   836→//     "user" = exclude jobs with user-created tags (not auto-tagger types "app"/"jobClass")
   837→//
   838→// Returns a slice of jobs or an error if the time range is invalid or query fails.
   839→func (r *JobRepository) FindJobsBetween(startTimeBegin int64, startTimeEnd int64, omitTagged string) ([]*schema.Job, error) {
   840→	var query sq.SelectBuilder
   841→
   842→	if startTimeBegin == startTimeEnd || startTimeBegin > startTimeEnd {
   843→		return nil, errors.New("startTimeBegin is equal or larger startTimeEnd")
   844→	}
   845→
   846→	if startTimeBegin == 0 {
   847→		cclog.Infof("Find jobs before %d", startTimeEnd)
   848→		query = sq.Select(jobColumns...).From("job").Where("job.start_time < ?", startTimeEnd)
   849→	} else {
   850→		cclog.Infof("Find jobs between %d and %d", startTimeBegin, startTimeEnd)
   851→		query = sq.Select(jobColumns...).From("job").Where("job.start_time BETWEEN ? AND ?", startTimeBegin, startTimeEnd)
   852→	}
   853→
   854→	switch omitTagged {
   855→	case "all":
   856→		query = query.Where("NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM jobtag WHERE jobtag.job_id = job.id)")
   857→	case "user":
   858→		query = query.Where("NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM jobtag JOIN tag ON tag.id = jobtag.tag_id WHERE jobtag.job_id = job.id AND tag.tag_type NOT IN ('app', 'jobClass'))")
   859→	}
   860→
   861→	query = query.OrderBy("job.cluster ASC", "job.subcluster ASC", "job.project ASC", "job.start_time ASC")
   862→
   863→	rows, err := query.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   864→	if err != nil {
   865→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running FindJobsBetween query: %v", err)
   866→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to find jobs between %d and %d: %w", startTimeBegin, startTimeEnd, err)
   867→	}
   868→	defer rows.Close()
   869→
   870→	jobs := make([]*schema.Job, 0, 50)
   871→	for rows.Next() {
   872→		job, err := scanJob(rows)
   873→		if err != nil {
   874→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning rows in FindJobsBetween: %v", err)
   875→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to scan job in FindJobsBetween: %w", err)
   876→		}
   877→		jobs = append(jobs, job)
   878→	}
   879→
   880→	cclog.Debugf("JobRepository.FindJobsBetween(): Return job count %d (omitTagged: %v)", len(jobs), omitTagged)
   881→	return jobs, nil
   882→}
   883→
   884→// UpdateMonitoringStatus updates the monitoring status for a job and invalidates its cache entries.
   885→// Cache invalidation affects both metadata and energy footprint to ensure consistency.
   886→//
   887→// Parameters:
   888→//   - job: Database ID of the job to update
   889→//   - monitoringStatus: New monitoring status value (see schema.MonitoringStatus constants)
   890→//
   891→// Returns an error if the database update fails.
   892→func (r *JobRepository) UpdateMonitoringStatus(job int64, monitoringStatus int32) (err error) {
   893→	// Invalidate cache entries as monitoring status affects job state
   894→	r.cache.Del(fmt.Sprintf("metadata:%d", job))
   895→	r.cache.Del(fmt.Sprintf("energyFootprint:%d", job))
   896→
   897→	stmt := sq.Update("job").
   898→		Set("monitoring_status", monitoringStatus).
   899→		Where("job.id = ?", job)
   900→
   901→	if _, err = stmt.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   902→		cclog.Errorf("Error while updating monitoring status for job %d: %v", job, err)
   903→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to update monitoring status for job %d: %w", job, err)
   904→	}
   905→	return nil
   906→}
   907→
   908→// Execute runs a Squirrel UpdateBuilder statement against the database.
   909→// This is a generic helper for executing pre-built update queries.
   910→//
   911→// Parameters:
   912→//   - stmt: Squirrel UpdateBuilder with prepared update query
   913→//
   914→// Returns an error if the execution fails.
   915→func (r *JobRepository) Execute(stmt sq.UpdateBuilder) error {
   916→	if _, err := stmt.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   917→		cclog.Errorf("Error while executing statement: %v", err)
   918→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to execute update statement: %w", err)
   919→	}
   920→
   921→	return nil
   922→}
   923→
   924→// MarkArchived adds monitoring status update to an existing UpdateBuilder statement.
   925→// This is a builder helper used when constructing multi-field update queries.
   926→//
   927→// Parameters:
   928→//   - stmt: Existing UpdateBuilder to modify
   929→//   - monitoringStatus: Monitoring status value to set
   930→//
   931→// Returns the modified UpdateBuilder for method chaining.
   932→func (r *JobRepository) MarkArchived(
   933→	stmt sq.UpdateBuilder,
   934→	monitoringStatus int32,
   935→) sq.UpdateBuilder {
   936→	return stmt.Set("monitoring_status", monitoringStatus)
   937→}

content.text:
Now I have the complete picture. Here are my findings:

## Root Cause Analysis

### 1. Missing `defer rows.Close()` in `user.go:GetLdapUsernames()` — real leak

content.content: The file /Users/jan/prg/CC/cc-backend/internal/repository/user.go has been updated successfully.

content.text:
### 2. Why the index isn't used — two separate problems

**Problem A: No ANALYZE statistics.** `sqlite_stat1` has zero rows for the `job` table. Without statistics, SQLite guesses row counts and picks `jobs_jobstate_energy` over the better `jobs_jobstate_starttime_duration`. Let me verify:

input.command: sqlite3 ./var/job.db "ANALYZE; EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN SELECT * FROM job WHERE job.job_state IN ('completed', 'running', 'failed') ORDER BY job.start_time DESC LIMIT 1 OFFSET 10;"
input.description: Check query plan AFTER running ANALYZE

content.content:
QUERY PLAN
`--SCAN job USING INDEX jobs_starttime

toolUseResult.stdout:
QUERY PLAN
`--SCAN job USING INDEX jobs_starttime

content.text:
After `ANALYZE`, SQLite switches to scanning `jobs_starttime` in reverse — **no temp B-tree sort**, no materializing all rows. This is the optimal plan: walk `start_time DESC`, check state filter per row, stop after LIMIT+OFFSET matches.

**Problem B: `IN` with multiple values prevents composite index sort.** Even with the right index `(job_state, start_time)`, an `IN` clause with 3 values forces SQLite to merge 3 index ranges, requiring a temp sort. But with ANALYZE, SQLite is smart enough to realize the states cover most rows and uses the `start_time`-only index instead — avoiding the sort entirely.

### 3. Missing `jobs_jobstate_starttime` index

Migration 08 created `jobs_jobstate_starttime ON (job_state, start_time)` but it's **missing from the actual database** — only `jobs_jobstate_starttime_duration` (3-column version from migration 09) exists. This is suspicious but less important now that ANALYZE resolves the planner issue.

### Fix: Run ANALYZE on startup

Let me find where the DB is initialized to add an ANALYZE call.

    14→	"github.com/golang-migrate/migrate/v4"
    15→	"github.com/golang-migrate/migrate/v4/database/sqlite3"
    16→	"github.com/golang-migrate/migrate/v4/source/iofs"
    17→)
    18→
    19→// Version is the current database schema version required by this version of cc-backend.
    20→// When the database schema changes, this version is incremented and a new migration file
    21→// is added to internal/repository/migrations/sqlite3/.
    22→//
    23→// Version history:
    24→//   - Version 10: Current version
    25→//
    26→// Migration files are embedded at build time from the migrations directory.
    27→const Version uint = 10
    28→
    29→//go:embed migrations/*
    30→var migrationFiles embed.FS
    31→
    32→// checkDBVersion verifies that the database schema version matches the expected version.
    33→// This is called automatically during Connect() to ensure schema compatibility.
    34→//
    35→// Returns an error if:
    36→//   - Database version is older than expected (needs migration)
    37→//   - Database version is newer than expected (needs app upgrade)
    38→//   - Database is in a dirty state (failed migration)
    39→//
    40→// A "dirty" database indicates a migration was started but not completed successfully.
    41→// This requires manual intervention to fix the database and force the version.
    42→func checkDBVersion(db *sql.DB) error {
    43→	driver, err := sqlite3.WithInstance(db, &sqlite3.Config{})
    44→	if err != nil {
    45→		return err
    46→	}
    47→	d, err := iofs.New(migrationFiles, "migrations/sqlite3")
    48→	if err != nil {
    49→		return err
    50→	}
    51→
    52→	m, err := migrate.NewWithInstance("iofs", d, "sqlite3", driver)
    53→	if err != nil {
    54→		return err
    55→	}
    56→
    57→	v, dirty, err := m.Version()
    58→	if err != nil {
    59→		if err == migrate.ErrNilVersion {
    60→			cclog.Warn("Legacy database without version or missing database file!")
    61→		} else {
    62→			return err
    63→		}
    64→	}
    65→
    66→	if v < Version {
    67→		return fmt.Errorf("unsupported database version %d, need %d.\nPlease backup your database file and run cc-backend -migrate-db", v, Version)
    68→	} else if v > Version {
    69→		return fmt.Errorf("unsupported database version %d, need %d.\nPlease refer to documentation how to downgrade db with external migrate tool", v, Version)
    70→	}
    71→
    72→	if dirty {
    73→		return fmt.Errorf("last migration to version %d has failed, please fix the db manually and force version with -force-db flag", Version)
    74→	}
    75→
    76→	return nil
    77→}
    78→
    79→// getMigrateInstance creates a new migration instance for the given database file.
    80→// This is used internally by MigrateDB, RevertDB, and ForceDB.
    81→func getMigrateInstance(db string) (m *migrate.Migrate, err error) {
    82→	d, err := iofs.New(migrationFiles, "migrations/sqlite3")
    83→	if err != nil {
    84→		return nil, err
    85→	}
    86→
    87→	m, err = migrate.NewWithSourceInstance("iofs", d, fmt.Sprintf("sqlite3://%s?_foreign_keys=on", db))
    88→	if err != nil {
    89→		return nil, err
    90→	}
    91→
    92→	return m, nil
    93→}
    94→
    95→// MigrateDB applies all pending database migrations to bring the schema up to date.
    96→// This should be run with the -migrate-db flag before starting the application
    97→// after upgrading to a new version that requires schema changes.
    98→//
    99→// Process:
   100→//  1. Checks current database version
   101→//  2. Applies all migrations from current version to target Version
   102→//  3. Updates schema_migrations table to track applied migrations
   103→//
   104→// Important:
   105→//   - Always backup your database before running migrations
   106→//   - Migrations are irreversible without manual intervention
   107→//   - If a migration fails, the database is marked "dirty" and requires manual fix
   108→//
   109→// Usage:
   110→//
   111→//	cc-backend -migrate-db
   112→func MigrateDB(db string) error {
   113→	m, err := getMigrateInstance(db)
   114→	if err != nil {
   115→		return err
   116→	}
   117→
   118→	v, dirty, err := m.Version()
   119→	if err != nil {
   120→		if err == migrate.ErrNilVersion {
   121→			cclog.Info("Legacy database without version or missing database file!")
   122→		} else {
   123→			return err
   124→		}
   125→	}
   126→
   127→	if v < Version {
   128→		cclog.Infof("unsupported database version %d, need %d.\nPlease backup your database file and run cc-backend -migrate-db", v, Version)
   129→	}
   130→
   131→	if dirty {
   132→		return fmt.Errorf("last migration to version %d has failed, please fix the db manually and force version with -force-db flag", Version)
   133→	}
   134→
   135→	if err := m.Up(); err != nil {
   136→		if err == migrate.ErrNoChange {
   137→			cclog.Info("DB already up to date!")
   138→		} else {
   139→			return err
   140→		}
   141→	}
   142→
   143→	m.Close()
   144→	return nil
   145→}
   146→
   147→// RevertDB rolls back the database schema to the previous version (Version - 1).
   148→// This is primarily used for testing or emergency rollback scenarios.
   149→//
   150→// Warning:
   151→//   - This may cause data loss if newer schema added columns/tables
   152→//   - Always backup before reverting
   153→//   - Not all migrations are safely reversible
   154→//
   155→// Usage:
   156→//
   157→//	cc-backend -revert-db
   158→func RevertDB(db string) error {
   159→	m, err := getMigrateInstance(db)
   160→	if err != nil {
   161→		return err
   162→	}
   163→
   164→	if err := m.Migrate(Version - 1); err != nil {
   165→		if err == migrate.ErrNoChange {
   166→			cclog.Info("DB already up to date!")
   167→		} else {
   168→			return err
   169→		}
   170→	}
   171→
   172→	m.Close()
   173→	return nil
   174→}
   175→
   176→// ForceDB forces the database schema version to the current Version without running migrations.
   177→// This is only used to recover from failed migrations that left the database in a "dirty" state.
   178→//
   179→// When to use:
   180→//   - After manually fixing a failed migration
   181→//   - When you've manually applied schema changes and need to update the version marker
   182→//
   183→// Warning:
   184→//   - This does NOT apply any schema changes
   185→//   - Only use after manually verifying the schema is correct
   186→//   - Improper use can cause schema/version mismatch
   187→//
   188→// Usage:
   189→//
   190→//	cc-backend -force-db
   191→func ForceDB(db string) error {
   192→	m, err := getMigrateInstance(db)
   193→	if err != nil {
   194→		return err
   195→	}
   196→
   197→	if err := m.Force(int(Version)); err != nil {
   198→		return err
   199→	}
   200→
   201→	m.Close()
   202→	return nil
   203→}
   204→

content.text: The right place to add ANALYZE is in `setupSqlite`, which runs once on connection. But ANALYZE on a large DB can take seconds, so it's better to also add it after migrations. Let me add it to both `setupSqlite` (runs every startup) and check the runtime impact:

lines: +  // Update query planner statistics so SQLite picks optimal indexes.
+  // Without this, SQLite guesses row distributions and often chooses wrong
+  // indexes for queries with IN clauses + ORDER BY, causing full table sorts
+  // in temp B-trees instead of using covering indexes.

content.text:
All passes. Here's a summary of findings and fixes:

---

## Findings

### Bug 1: Missing `defer rows.Close()` in `user.go:GetLdapUsernames()` (line 100)
**Fixed.** Every call leaked a database connection and its buffers.

### Bug 2: SQLite never runs `ANALYZE` — wrong index selection
**Fixed.** Added `ANALYZE` to `setupSqlite()` in `dbConnection.go`.

Without statistics in `sqlite_stat1`, SQLite's query planner **guessed** and chose `jobs_jobstate_energy` (a terrible index for this query) instead of scanning `jobs_starttime` in reverse. The result:

| | Before ANALYZE | After ANALYZE |
|---|---|---|
| Index used | `jobs_jobstate_energy` | `jobs_starttime` |
| Sort | **TEMP B-TREE** (materializes ALL rows) | None (index order) |
| Memory | Proportional to total matching rows | Constant |
| I/O | Full scan of all matching rows | Stops at OFFSET+LIMIT |

### Bug 3: `IN` clause + `ORDER BY` is fundamentally incompatible with composite indexes

Even with the "correct" index `(job_state, start_time)`, SQLite **cannot** merge-sort across 3 separate index range scans for `IN ('completed','running','failed')`. It always falls back to a temp B-tree sort. The only efficient plan is to use the standalone `jobs_starttime` index — which SQLite does automatically **after ANALYZE** because it realizes the 3 states cover virtually all rows, making the WHERE clause nearly a no-op.

### Observation: 79 indexes on the `job` table
This is excessive and actively harmful — it confuses the query planner (especially without ANALYZE) and slows writes. The `jobs_jobstate_starttime` index from migration 08 is also missing from the actual DB (only the 3-column `jobs_jobstate_starttime_duration` exists). This is worth investigating separately but is a schema/migration concern, not a code bug.

message.content: What optimal index selection do you suggest? The job table contains almost 20 million jobs in production causing timouts on any filter query that does not use and index.

file.content:
# Fix Missing `rows.Close()` Memory Leaks in SQLite3 Queries

## Context

Production memory leaks traced to queries that do full table scans (e.g., job state list sorted by `start_time` on all jobs). The root cause is `sql.Rows` objects not being closed after query execution. In Go's `database/sql`, every `rows` returned by `.Query()` holds a database connection and associated buffers until `rows.Close()` is called. Without `defer rows.Close()`, these leak on every code path (both success and error returns).

## Part 1: Missing `defer rows.Close()` (16 locations)

**22 total `.Query()` calls** across the repository layer. **15 have `defer rows.Close()`**. **7 do not** (plus additional in tags/node). Additionally, 1 `Queryx` call in `tags.go` is also missing close.

### 1a. `internal/repository/stats.go` — 7 functions

Add `defer rows.Close()` immediately after the `if err != nil` check for each:

| Line | Function |
|------|----------|
| 237 | `JobsStatsGrouped` |
| 442 | `JobCountGrouped` |
| 498 | `AddJobCountGrouped` |
| 557 | `AddJobCount` |
| 757 | `jobsStatisticsHistogram` |
| 825 | `jobsDurationStatisticsHistogram` |
| 950 | `jobsMetricStatisticsHistogram` |

### 1b. `internal/repository/tags.go` — 5 locations

| Line | Function | Detail |
|------|----------|--------|
| 284 | `CountTags` | Add `defer xrows.Close()` after Queryx error check |
| 336 | `CountTags` | Add `defer rows.Close()` after Query error check |
| 513 | `GetTags` | Add `defer rows.Close()` |
| 546 | `GetTagsDirect` | Add `defer rows.Close()` |
| 584 | `getArchiveTags` | Add `defer rows.Close()` |

### 1c. `internal/repository/node.go` — 4 functions

Replace manual `rows.Close()` in error-only paths with `defer rows.Close()`:

| Line | Function | Also remove manual close at |
|------|----------|----|
| 368 | `QueryNodes` | L375 |
| 417 | `QueryNodesWithMeta` | L427 |
| 563 | `CountStates` | L569 |
| 625 | `CountStatesTimed` | L633 |

## Part 2: Missing `rows.Err()` Checks

After a `rows.Next()` loop, `rows.Err()` should be checked to detect iteration errors (e.g., connection lost mid-scan). Currently only 3 of ~22 query sites check it.

Add `rows.Err()` check after the scan loop in every function touched by Part 1. Pattern:

```go
for rows.Next() {
    // ... scan ...
}
if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
    return nil, err
}
```

Functions needing this (same 16 locations as Part 1 — all functions listed above).

## Part 3: Unbounded Query Optimization

### 3a. `CountStates` in `node.go:546` — counting in Go instead of SQL

Currently fetches every individual row (`SELECT column FROM node ...`) and counts in Go with a map. Should use SQL aggregation instead:

```go
// BEFORE: fetches N rows, counts in Go
sq.Select(column).From("node")...

// AFTER: let SQL do the counting
sq.Select(column, "COUNT(*) as count").From("node")...GroupBy(column)
```

This changes the scan from `rows.Scan(&state)` to `rows.Scan(&state, &count)` and eliminates the Go-side counting loop. Reduces rows transferred from N (number of nodes) to ~5 (number of distinct states).

### 3b. `AddJobCount` in `stats.go:541` — uses `.Query()` instead of `.QueryRow()`

`AddJobCount` builds a count query without GROUP BY (returns a single row) but uses `.Query()` + loop instead of `.QueryRow()`. Should be simplified to `QueryRow().Scan()` like `JobsStats` does. This eliminates the rows object entirely.

## Summary of All Edits

| File | Function | Actions |
|------|----------|---------|
| `stats.go` | `JobsStatsGrouped` | + `defer rows.Close()`, + `rows.Err()` |
| `stats.go` | `JobCountGrouped` | + `defer rows.Close()`, + `rows.Err()` |
| `stats.go` | `AddJobCountGrouped` | + `defer rows.Close()`, + `rows.Err()` |
| `stats.go` | `AddJobCount` | Rewrite to use `QueryRow` instead of `Query` |
| `stats.go` | `jobsStatisticsHistogram` | + `defer rows.Close()`, + `rows.Err()` |
| `stats.go` | `jobsDurationStatisticsHistogram` | + `defer rows.Close()`, + `rows.Err()` |
| `stats.go` | `jobsMetricStatisticsHistogram` | + `defer rows.Close()`, + `rows.Err()` |
| `tags.go` | `CountTags` (xrows) | + `defer xrows.Close()`, + `xrows.Err()` after loop |
| `tags.go` | `CountTags` (rows) | + `defer rows.Close()` (already has `rows.Err()`) |
| `tags.go` | `GetTags` | + `defer rows.Close()`, + `rows.Err()` |
| `tags.go` | `GetTagsDirect` | + `defer rows.Close()`, + `rows.Err()` |
| `tags.go` | `getArchiveTags` | + `defer rows.Close()`, + `rows.Err()` |
| `node.go` | `QueryNodes` | + `defer rows.Close()`, remove manual close, + `rows.Err()` |
| `node.go` | `QueryNodesWithMeta` | + `defer rows.Close()`, remove manual close, + `rows.Err()` |
| `node.go` | `CountStates` | Rewrite to use SQL `COUNT(*)...GROUP BY`, + `defer rows.Close()`, remove manual close |
| `node.go` | `CountStatesTimed` | + `defer rows.Close()`, remove manual close, + `rows.Err()` |

## Verification

1. `go build ./...` — ensure it compiles
2. `go test ./internal/repository/...` — run repository tests
3. `go vet ./internal/repository/...` — static analysis

    20→// Find executes a SQL query to find a specific batch job.
    21→// The job is queried using the batch job id, the cluster name,
    22→// and the start time of the job in UNIX epoch time seconds.
    23→// It returns a pointer to a schema.Job data structure and an error variable.
    24→// To check if no job was found test err == sql.ErrNoRows
    25→func (r *JobRepository) Find(
    26→	jobID *int64,
    27→	cluster *string,
    28→	startTime *int64,
    29→) (*schema.Job, error) {
    30→	if jobID == nil {
    31→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("jobID cannot be nil")
    32→	}
    33→
    34→	start := time.Now()
    35→	q := sq.Select(jobColumns...).From("job").
    36→		Where("job.job_id = ?", *jobID)
    37→
    38→	if cluster != nil {
    39→		q = q.Where("job.cluster = ?", *cluster)
    40→	}
    41→	if startTime != nil {
    42→		q = q.Where("job.start_time = ?", *startTime)
    43→	}
    44→
    45→	q = q.OrderBy("job.id DESC").Limit(1) // always use newest matching job by db id if more than one match
    46→
    47→	cclog.Debugf("Timer Find %s", time.Since(start))
    48→	return scanJob(q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).QueryRow())
    49→}

    81→// FindAll executes a SQL query to find all batch jobs matching the given criteria.
    82→// Jobs are queried using the batch job id, and optionally filtered by cluster name
    83→// and start time (UNIX epoch time seconds).
    84→// It returns a slice of pointers to schema.Job data structures and an error variable.
    85→// An empty slice is returned if no matching jobs are found.
    86→func (r *JobRepository) FindAll(
    87→	jobID *int64,
    88→	cluster *string,
    89→	startTime *int64,
    90→) ([]*schema.Job, error) {
    91→	if jobID == nil {
    92→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("jobID cannot be nil")
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	start := time.Now()
    96→	q := sq.Select(jobColumns...).From("job").
    97→		Where("job.job_id = ?", *jobID)
    98→
    99→	if cluster != nil {
   100→		q = q.Where("job.cluster = ?", *cluster)
   101→	}
   102→	if startTime != nil {
   103→		q = q.Where("job.start_time = ?", *startTime)
   104→	}
   105→
   106→	rows, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   107→	if err != nil {
   108→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running FindAll query for jobID=%d: %v", *jobID, err)
   109→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to execute FindAll query: %w", err)
   110→	}
   111→	defer rows.Close()
   112→
   113→	jobs := make([]*schema.Job, 0, 10)
   114→	for rows.Next() {
   115→		job, err := scanJob(rows)
   116→		if err != nil {
   117→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning rows in FindAll: %v", err)
   118→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to scan job row: %w", err)
   119→		}
   120→		jobs = append(jobs, job)
   121→	}
   122→	cclog.Debugf("Timer FindAll %s", time.Since(start))
   123→	return jobs, nil
   124→}

   126→// GetJobList returns job IDs for non-running jobs.
   127→// This is useful to process large job counts and intended to be used
   128→// together with FindById to process jobs one by one.
   129→// Use limit and offset for pagination. Use limit=0 to get all results (not recommended for large datasets).
   130→func (r *JobRepository) GetJobList(limit int, offset int) ([]int64, error) {
   131→	query := sq.Select("id").From("job").
   132→		Where("job.job_state != 'running'")
   133→
   134→	// Add pagination if limit is specified
   135→	if limit > 0 {
   136→		query = query.Limit(uint64(limit)).Offset(uint64(offset))
   137→	}
   138→
   139→	rows, err := query.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   140→	if err != nil {
   141→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running GetJobList query (limit=%d, offset=%d): %v", limit, offset, err)
   142→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to execute GetJobList query: %w", err)
   143→	}
   144→	defer rows.Close()
   145→
   146→	jl := make([]int64, 0, 1000)
   147→	for rows.Next() {
   148→		var id int64
   149→		err := rows.Scan(&id)
   150→		if err != nil {
   151→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning rows in GetJobList: %v", err)
   152→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to scan job ID: %w", err)
   153→		}
   154→		jl = append(jl, id)
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	cclog.Debugf("JobRepository.GetJobList(): Return job count %d", len(jl))
   158→	return jl, nil
   159→}

   223→// IsJobOwner checks if the specified user owns the batch job identified by jobID,
   224→// startTime, and cluster. Returns true if the user is the owner, false otherwise.
   225→// This method does not return errors; it returns false for both non-existent jobs
   226→// and jobs owned by other users.
   227→func (r *JobRepository) IsJobOwner(jobID int64, startTime int64, user string, cluster string) bool {
   228→	q := sq.Select("id").
   229→		From("job").
   230→		Where("job.job_id = ?", jobID).
   231→		Where("job.hpc_user = ?", user).
   232→		Where("job.cluster = ?", cluster).
   233→		Where("job.start_time = ?", startTime)
   234→
   235→	_, err := scanJob(q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).QueryRow())
   236→	if err != nil && err != sql.ErrNoRows {
   237→		cclog.Warnf("IsJobOwner: unexpected error for jobID=%d, user=%s, cluster=%s: %v", jobID, user, cluster, err)
   238→	}
   239→	return err != sql.ErrNoRows
   240→}

   242→func (r *JobRepository) FindConcurrentJobs(
   243→	ctx context.Context,
   244→	job *schema.Job,
   245→) (*model.JobLinkResultList, error) {
   246→	if job == nil {
   247→		return nil, nil
   248→	}
   249→
   250→	query, qerr := SecurityCheck(ctx, sq.Select("job.id", "job.job_id", "job.start_time").From("job"))
   251→	if qerr != nil {
   252→		return nil, qerr
   253→	}
   254→
   255→	query = query.Where("cluster = ?", job.Cluster)
   256→
   257→	if len(job.Resources) == 0 {
   258→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("job has no resources defined")
   259→	}
   260→
   261→	var startTime int64
   262→	var stopTime int64
   263→
   264→	startTime = job.StartTime
   265→	hostname := job.Resources[0].Hostname
   266→
   267→	if job.State == schema.JobStateRunning {
   268→		stopTime = time.Now().Unix()
   269→	} else {
   270→		stopTime = startTime + int64(job.Duration)
   271→	}
   272→
   273→	// Time buffer constant for finding overlapping jobs
   274→	// overlapBufferEnd: 200s buffer at job end to account for scheduling/cleanup overlap
   275→	const overlapBufferEnd = 200
   276→
   277→	stopTimeTail := stopTime - overlapBufferEnd
   278→	startTimeFront := startTime + overlapBufferEnd
   279→
   280→	queryRunning := query.Where("job.job_state = ?", "running").
   281→		Where("job.start_time <= ?", stopTimeTail)
   282→	// Get At Least One Exact Hostname Match from JSON Resources Array in Database
   283→	queryRunning = queryRunning.Where("EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM json_each(job.resources) WHERE json_extract(value, '$.hostname') = ?)", hostname)
   284→
   285→	query = query.Where("job.job_state != ?", "running").
   286→		Where("job.start_time < ?", stopTimeTail).
   287→		Where("(job.start_time + job.duration) > ?", startTimeFront)
   288→	// Get At Least One Exact Hostname Match from JSON Resources Array in Database
   289→	query = query.Where("EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM json_each(job.resources) WHERE json_extract(value, '$.hostname') = ?)", hostname)
   290→
   291→	rows, err := query.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   292→	if err != nil {
   293→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running concurrent jobs query: %v", err)
   294→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to execute concurrent jobs query: %w", err)
   295→	}
   296→	defer rows.Close()
   297→
   298→	items := make([]*model.JobLink, 0, 10)
   299→	queryString := fmt.Sprintf("cluster=%s", job.Cluster)
   300→
   301→	for rows.Next() {
   302→		var id, jobID, startTime sql.NullInt64
   303→
   304→		if err = rows.Scan(&id, &jobID, &startTime); err != nil {
   305→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning concurrent job rows: %v", err)
   306→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to scan concurrent job row: %w", err)
   307→		}
   308→
   309→		if id.Valid {
   310→			queryString += fmt.Sprintf("&jobId=%d", int(jobID.Int64))
   311→			items = append(items,
   312→				&model.JobLink{
   313→					ID:    fmt.Sprint(id.Int64),
   314→					JobID: int(jobID.Int64),
   315→				})
   316→		}
   317→	}
   318→
   319→	rows, err = queryRunning.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   320→	if err != nil {
   321→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running concurrent running jobs query: %v", err)
   322→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to execute concurrent running jobs query: %w", err)
   323→	}
   324→	defer rows.Close()
   325→
   326→	for rows.Next() {
   327→		var id, jobID, startTime sql.NullInt64
   328→
   329→		if err := rows.Scan(&id, &jobID, &startTime); err != nil {
   330→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning running concurrent job rows: %v", err)
   331→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to scan running concurrent job row: %w", err)
   332→		}
   333→
   334→		if id.Valid {
   335→			queryString += fmt.Sprintf("&jobId=%d", int(jobID.Int64))
   336→			items = append(items,
   337→				&model.JobLink{
   338→					ID:    fmt.Sprint(id.Int64),
   339→					JobID: int(jobID.Int64),
   340→				})
   341→		}
   342→	}
   343→
   344→	cnt := len(items)
   345→
   346→	return &model.JobLinkResultList{
   347→		ListQuery: &queryString,
   348→		Items:     items,
   349→		Count:     &cnt,
   350→	}, nil
   351→}

    19→	shared, monitoring_status, smt, job_state, start_time, duration, walltime, footprint, energy, energy_footprint, resources, meta_data

    25→const NamedJobInsert string = `INSERT INTO job (

    27→	shared, monitoring_status, smt, job_state, start_time, duration, walltime, footprint, energy, energy_footprint, resources, meta_data

    33→// InsertJobDirect inserts a job directly into the job table (not job_cache).
    34→// Use this when the returned ID will be used for operations on the job table
    35→// (e.g., adding tags), or for imported jobs that are already completed.
    36→func (r *JobRepository) InsertJobDirect(job *schema.Job) (int64, error) {
    37→	r.Mutex.Lock()
    38→	defer r.Mutex.Unlock()
    39→
    40→	res, err := r.DB.NamedExec(NamedJobInsert, job)
    41→	if err != nil {
    42→		cclog.Warn("Error while NamedJobInsert (direct)")
    43→		return 0, err
    44→	}
    45→	id, err := res.LastInsertId()
    46→	if err != nil {
    47→		cclog.Warn("Error while getting last insert ID (direct)")
    48→		return 0, err
    49→	}
    50→
    51→	return id, nil
    52→}

    72→func (r *JobRepository) SyncJobs() ([]*schema.Job, error) {
    73→	r.Mutex.Lock()
    74→	defer r.Mutex.Unlock()
    75→
    76→	query := sq.Select(jobCacheColumns...).From("job_cache")
    77→
    78→	rows, err := query.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
    79→	if err != nil {
    80→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running query %v", err)
    81→		return nil, err
    82→	}
    83→	defer rows.Close()
    84→
    85→	jobs := make([]*schema.Job, 0, 50)
    86→	for rows.Next() {
    87→		job, err := scanJob(rows)
    88→		if err != nil {
    89→			cclog.Warn("Error while scanning rows")
    90→			return nil, err
    91→		}
    92→		jobs = append(jobs, job)
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	// Use INSERT OR IGNORE to skip jobs already transferred by the stop path
    96→	_, err = r.DB.Exec(
    97→		"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO job (job_id, cluster, subcluster, start_time, hpc_user, project, cluster_partition, array_job_id, num_nodes, num_hwthreads, num_acc, shared, monitoring_status, smt, job_state, duration, walltime, footprint, energy, energy_footprint, resources, meta_data) SELECT job_id, cluster, subcluster, start_time, hpc_user, project, cluster_partition, array_job_id, num_nodes, num_hwthreads, num_acc, shared, monitoring_status, smt, job_state, duration, walltime, footprint, energy, energy_footprint, resources, meta_data FROM job_cache")
    98→	if err != nil {
    99→		cclog.Warnf("Error while Job sync: %v", err)
   100→		return nil, err
   101→	}
   102→
   103→	_, err = r.DB.Exec("DELETE FROM job_cache")
   104→	if err != nil {
   105→		cclog.Warnf("Error while Job cache clean: %v", err)
   106→		return nil, err
   107→	}
   108→
   109→	// Resolve correct job.id from the job table. The IDs read from job_cache
   110→	// are from a different auto-increment sequence and must not be used to
   111→	// query the job table.
   112→	for _, job := range jobs {
   113→		var newID int64
   114→		if err := sq.Select("job.id").From("job").
   115→			Where("job.job_id = ? AND job.cluster = ? AND job.start_time = ?",
   116→				job.JobID, job.Cluster, job.StartTime).
   117→			RunWith(r.stmtCache).QueryRow().Scan(&newID); err != nil {
   118→			cclog.Warnf("SyncJobs: could not resolve job table id for job %d on %s: %v",
   119→				job.JobID, job.Cluster, err)
   120→			continue
   121→		}
   122→		job.ID = &newID
   123→	}
   124→
   125→	return jobs, nil
   126→}

   128→// TransferCachedJobToMain moves a job from job_cache to the job table.
   129→// Caller must hold r.Mutex. Returns the new job table ID.
   130→func (r *JobRepository) TransferCachedJobToMain(cacheID int64) (int64, error) {
   131→	res, err := r.DB.Exec(
   132→		"INSERT INTO job (job_id, cluster, subcluster, start_time, hpc_user, project, cluster_partition, array_job_id, num_nodes, num_hwthreads, num_acc, shared, monitoring_status, smt, job_state, duration, walltime, footprint, energy, energy_footprint, resources, meta_data) SELECT job_id, cluster, subcluster, start_time, hpc_user, project, cluster_partition, array_job_id, num_nodes, num_hwthreads, num_acc, shared, monitoring_status, smt, job_state, duration, walltime, footprint, energy, energy_footprint, resources, meta_data FROM job_cache WHERE id = ?",
   133→		cacheID)
   134→	if err != nil {
   135→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("transferring cached job %d to main table failed: %w", cacheID, err)
   136→	}
   137→
   138→	newID, err := res.LastInsertId()
   139→	if err != nil {
   140→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("getting new job ID after transfer failed: %w", err)
   141→	}
   142→
   143→	_, err = r.DB.Exec("DELETE FROM job_cache WHERE id = ?", cacheID)
   144→	if err != nil {
   145→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("deleting cached job %d after transfer failed: %w", cacheID, err)
   146→	}
   147→
   148→	return newID, nil
   149→}
   150→
   151→// Start inserts a new job in the table, returning the unique job ID.
   152→// Statistics are not transfered!
   153→func (r *JobRepository) Start(job *schema.Job) (id int64, err error) {
   154→	job.RawFootprint, err = json.Marshal(job.Footprint)
   155→	if err != nil {
   156→		return -1, fmt.Errorf("REPOSITORY/JOB > encoding footprint field failed: %w", err)
   157→	}
   158→
   159→	job.RawResources, err = json.Marshal(job.Resources)
   160→	if err != nil {
   161→		return -1, fmt.Errorf("REPOSITORY/JOB > encoding resources field failed: %w", err)
   162→	}
   163→
   164→	job.RawMetaData, err = json.Marshal(job.MetaData)
   165→	if err != nil {
   166→		return -1, fmt.Errorf("REPOSITORY/JOB > encoding metaData field failed: %w", err)
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	return r.InsertJob(job)
   170→}
   171→
   172→// StartDirect inserts a new job directly into the job table (not job_cache).
   173→// Use this when the returned ID will immediately be used for job table
   174→// operations such as adding tags.
   175→func (r *JobRepository) StartDirect(job *schema.Job) (id int64, err error) {
   176→	job.RawFootprint, err = json.Marshal(job.Footprint)
   177→	if err != nil {
   178→		return -1, fmt.Errorf("REPOSITORY/JOB > encoding footprint field failed: %w", err)
   179→	}
   180→
   181→	job.RawResources, err = json.Marshal(job.Resources)
   182→	if err != nil {
   183→		return -1, fmt.Errorf("REPOSITORY/JOB > encoding resources field failed: %w", err)
   184→	}
   185→
   186→	job.RawMetaData, err = json.Marshal(job.MetaData)
   187→	if err != nil {
   188→		return -1, fmt.Errorf("REPOSITORY/JOB > encoding metaData field failed: %w", err)
   189→	}
   190→
   191→	return r.InsertJobDirect(job)
   192→}
   193→
   194→// Stop updates the job with the database id jobId using the provided arguments.
   195→func (r *JobRepository) Stop(
   196→	jobID int64,
   197→	duration int32,
   198→	state schema.JobState,
   199→	monitoringStatus int32,
   200→) (err error) {
   201→	// Invalidate cache entries as job state is changing
   202→	r.cache.Del(fmt.Sprintf("metadata:%d", jobID))
   203→	r.cache.Del(fmt.Sprintf("energyFootprint:%d", jobID))
   204→
   205→	stmt := sq.Update("job").
   206→		Set("job_state", state).
   207→		Set("duration", duration).
   208→		Set("monitoring_status", monitoringStatus).
   209→		Where("job.id = ?", jobID)
   210→
   211→	_, err = stmt.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec()
   212→	return err
   213→}
   214→
   215→
